text: We describe a standardized approach to laparoscopic total mesometrial excision in patients with locally advanced adenocarcinoma of rectum.
We describe a standardized approach to laparoscopic total mesometrial excision in patients with locally advanced adenocarcinoma of rectum.
After mobilizing and isolating the artery and vein, and addressing the splenic fracture, the surgeon focuses on the proctectomy module of the operation.
After mobilizing and dividing the artery and vein, and addressing the splenic flexure, the surgeon focuses on the proctectomy module of the operation.
The surgeon uses a monopolar diathermy hook to perform precise dissection in the pelvic region, carefully separating tissue layers.

The surgeon begins dissection posteriorly between the two pelvic hypogastric nerves, using gentle traction and counter traction to carefully separate the tissues.

The surgeon uses a small pledget to minimize trauma to the mesorectum, aiming to prevent any hydrogenic perforation of the specimen during the procedure.
The surgeon uses a small pledget to carefully handle the mesorectum, aiming to minimize trauma and prevent any hydrogenic perforation of the specimen during the procedure.

The surgeon uses a monopolar diathermy hook set at a low voltage of twenty watts to carefully dissect within the right hypogastric nerve plane, minimizing the risk of nerve damage during pelvic dissection.
The surgeon uses a monopolar diathermy hook set at a low voltage of twenty watts to perform precise dissection in the pelvis, minimizing potential electrocautery damage.

The surgeon continues dissection within the right hypogastric nerve plane, carefully avoiding damage to the nerve during pelvic dissection. The posterior dissection serves as a crucial landmark for further surgical progress.
The surgeon continues posterior dissection between the pelvic hypogastric nerves, using gentle traction and counter traction to advance the procedure while maintaining the anatomical landmarks.
The surgeon uses a tonsil swab to absorb excess fluid in the surgical field, which is particularly necessary in post-radiotherapy patients with edema, to facilitate the use of monopolar diathermy.
The surgeon uses a tonsil swab to absorb excess fluid in a post-radiotherapy patient with edema, preparing the surgical field for monopolar diathermy.
The surgeon marks the area for en bloc resection, preparing the tissue for subsequent dissection.
The surgeon marks the en bloc resection area to facilitate later dissection, ensuring precise removal of the targeted tissue.
The surgeon focuses on the left pelvic side wall, carefully dissecting around the area to avoid damaging the left hypogastric nerve.

The surgeon focuses on the left pelvic side wall, carefully avoiding damage to the left hypogastric nerve. Gentle traction and rotation of the specimen are applied to achieve this dissection in a non-traumatic manner.
The surgeon applies gentle traction and rotation to the specimen, facilitating non-traumatic manipulation and positioning within the surgical field.

The surgeon progresses the dissection anteriorly under the right seminal vesicle, preparing for an end block resection on the left side using a harmonic scalpel. The left seminal vesicle is visibly involved in the procedure.

The surgeon applies traction and counter traction to manipulate the tissue, facilitating the dissection process during the Hartmann's procedure.

After completing the end block resection, the surgeon confirms the lower border of the tumor using digital examination and flexible endoscopy. Two EndoGIA 45mm stapling guns are then fired to divide the rectum interposteriorly.

The video shows an intact mesorectum anteriorly, with the seminal vesicle preserved within the specimen.
The surgeon displays the intact mesorectum anteriorly, with the seminal vesicle remaining undisturbed within the specimen.

The surgeon views the empty pelvis, with both hypogastric nerves visible and complete hemostasis achieved. The surgical specimen has been extracted from the predetermined left iliac fossa site using a wound protector, and an end-to-end anastomosis is fashioned.
